methods.description: This method involves sequencing the genome of Serinicoccus sp., performing quality control on the reads, and annotating the genome to identify genes and subsystems.

steps.description: The genome sequence of Serinicoccus sp. showed a raw data set of 7,232,952 reads.
The data filtered by quality control showed 7,217,551 clean reads.
steps.searchable_summary: perform quality control on genome sequence data

steps.description: Serinicoccus sp. genome was found to have a total length of 3,503,838 paired-end reads.
Annotation analysis has identified 3368 genes, including 3303 coding genes, three rRNAs and 45 tRNAs.
Annotation by RAST assigned these genes into 264 subsystems.
steps.searchable_summary: assign genes to subsystems using RAST

methods.description: This method uses genomic indices such as DNA-DNA hybridisation, average nucleotide identity (ANI), OrthoANI, and amino acid identity (AAI) to determine species delineation of Serinicoccus sp.

steps.description: DNA-DNA hybridisation is widely used besides average nucleotide identity (ANI) and OrthoANI.
steps.searchable_summary: perform DNA-DNA hybridisation and ANI analysis

steps.description: The heat map from OAT software shows less than 85% similarity between their genome and other Serinicoccus species.
steps.searchable_summary: generate heat map using OAT software

steps.description: The DDH values for strain P2D13-UKM species showed <50% compared to all the genomes analysed.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate DDH values for species comparison

steps.description: Further analysis using amino acid identity (AAI) value to measure the relatedness of the shared genes.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate AAI values for gene relatedness
